mechanism: Extract LP dual values on binding PTDF flow constraints; accumulate causally via EMA per line; discount/boost each battery's continuation value proportional to its own PTDF exposure to historically-expensive lines
failure_reason: Best observed gain (+0.0008 at near-zero beta) is below the +0.003 promotion threshold; any larger magnitude in either direction actively regresses both scenarios
remaining_opportunity: None identified for this specific mechanism; the aggregate uniform congestion discount already active in best_candidate.py appears to already capture the exploitable portion of this signal

architecture: Designated-reliever joint SOC-diversity (sparse cluster-role preservation, arch #14/16/17 variant)
mechanism: Identify the single most-utilized line from public exogenous injections; give a bonus to only the one battery best-positioned (PTDF-sign-matched, most useful headroom) to relieve it -- sparse vs. architecture 1's dense per-battery adjustment
failure_reason: Negative at every tested scale, monotonically worse with larger beta; no positive regime found
remaining_opportunity: None identified; consistent with ARCH-01's finding that battery-specific network-topology-aware value corrections do not add exploitable information beyond the existing aggregate discount

note: Architectures 1-11, 15, 18-20 were not implemented -- scored and eliminated by direct application of two facts proven experimentally this session: (a) OA-O0=0 (the LP allocator is already exactly optimal given the current continuation value, so no re-solve/search/planning method operating on the SAME objective can improve on it), and (b) RT price shocks are proven i.i.d. with a fresh reseed every step (source-traced), so no forecasting/lookahead method can exceed the exact expectation already computed without illegal future information. See architecture_report.md for the full per-architecture table and reasoning.
